info.title: Nebula API
info.description: API served by the Nebula framework

post.summary:
Sign in with a username or email and password. No tenant header is
needed: the tenant is resolved from the credentials via the login
directory. A header (or single-tenant mode) skips resolution and
authenticates against that context directly — which is also how the
client answers a `tenant_selection` response.

get.summary: The caller's own effective permissions — any authenticated user.

post.summary:
Changing the password rotates the security stamp and revokes all
refresh tokens — every other session has to sign in again.

get.summary: The full permission definition tree, for admin UIs.

post.summary:
Company registration: the email and password provided here become the
tenant's admin account, seeded with the static `Admin` role.

Stores the logo at `{files.root}/{slug}/{id}/logo.{ext}`; it is then
served from the `logo_url` in the profile response.

Queue a background migration of the caller's tenant database — how a
tenant picks up newly deployed features without waiting for the next
restart. Needs `jobs.enabled`.

get.summary:
Readable by any authenticated user of the tenant — the company name,
logo and currency are what its own screens display.

The current company-wide 2FA policy. Readable by any authenticated
user of the tenant — the mandate is what they experience at sign-in,
and the profile page needs it to know whether opting out is possible.

post.summary: Company-wide policy switch; requires the tenant-settings permission.

Exchange a refresh token for a fresh access/refresh pair. The old
token is revoked; reusing it later revokes every session of the user.
Team onboarding; requires the user-creation permission.

put.summary:
Admin rights start with whoever registered the company; this grants
or revokes the static `Admin` role (which implicitly holds every
permission) for anyone later. Admins cannot demote themselves, so a
tenant can never lose its last admin by accident.
Replace a user's per-user overrides. Like roles, never on yourself.

Replace a user's role set. Changing your own roles is refused — an
admin locking themselves out is never one call away.

get.summary:
Anonymous on purpose: the onboarding form needs the list before a
tenant or user exists.

post.summary: Deployment-wide: the currency joins every tenant's picker.

parameters.name: warehouse_id
parameters.description: Restrict to one warehouse; otherwise all warehouses summed.

parameters.name: include_empty
parameters.description: Include exhausted lots (zero on hand). Default false.

parameters.name: warehouse_id
parameters.description: Matches either side of the document.

parameters.name: after_seq
parameters.description: Page cursor: rows with seq greater than this.

parameters.name: below_reorder
parameters.description: Only positions at or below their effective reorder level.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Document date window, inclusive; open-ended when omitted.

balance.description: Running balance on the account's normal side.

AccountType.description:
The five account classes of double-entry bookkeeping. Each one fixes
whether the account increases on the debit or the credit side.

currency.description: ISO 4217 code the account is denominated in.

is_system.description:
Seeded by the platform's default chart of accounts; cannot be
deleted (but may be renamed or deactivated).

system_key.description:
Stable platform role (e.g. `ar`, `vat_output`, `sales`) other
modules resolve this account by; `None` for user-created accounts.

exchange_rate.description: Required when the order currency differs from the base currency.

created_at.description:
The sea_orm alias hides the chrono type from utoipa's derive, so
the schema type is spelled out.

id.description:
Row id: the trail is an append-only sequence, so this stays a
bigserial — audit rows are log lines, not domain entities.

message.description: Human-readable line for `event` rows ("boss logged in").

request_id.description: The `x-request-id` of the request, linking audit rows to traces.

warehouse_id.description: Defaults from the linked requisition's warehouse.

BalanceSheet.description:
The balance sheet as of a date. The still-open nominal accounts' net
income is folded into equity so the sheet balances: `prior_earnings` is
income from fiscal years before the one covering the reference date
(retained earnings), `current_earnings` the current year's.

BatchLevelView.description: One lot's position, FEFO-ordered when expiries exist.

CompanyProfileResponse.description:
The tenant's company profile as shown to its users and edited in
tenant settings.

logo_url.description: Where the uploaded company logo is served from, when one exists.

tax_pin.description: Tax registration PIN (e.g. a KRA PIN).

recovery_codes.description: Shown exactly once — store them somewhere safe.

supplier_id.description: The supplier the draft purchase order goes to.

CostingMethod.description:
How issues out of stock are costed. The schema knows all three; only
moving average is implemented, so the others are rejected at the door
until their phases land.

currency.description: ISO 4217 code the account is denominated in.

code.description: Three uppercase ASCII letters (ISO 4217 or an app-defined unit).

minor_units.description: Decimal places of the minor unit, 0-6.

currency.description: ISO 4217 code; every line's account must be in this currency.

start_date.description: First day of the year; must be the first day of a month.

supplier_invoice_no.description: The supplier's document number — one entry per document.

from_warehouse_id.description:
Source warehouse: issues and transfers; for adjustments, the
warehouse being counted.

exchange_rate.description: Overrides the order's rate for this receipt only.

requisition_id.description: Copy this requisition's lines when `lines` is empty.

code.description: ISO 4217 code (or an app-defined unit), three uppercase letters.

is_system.description: Seeded rows are system currencies and cannot be deleted.

minor_units.description: Decimal places of the minor unit (KES/USD 2, JPY 0, BHD 3).

EntryStatus.description: Where a journal entry is in its lifecycle.

status.description:
Derived: `locked` if every period is locked, `closed` if none is open,
otherwise `open`.

GlReconciliationView.description:
The stock world vs. the GL world, side by side. The GL columns are
`None` when no accounting schema lives in this database.

grni_account_balance.description: Credit balance of the GRNI account.

grni_open.description: The operational GRNI position (received not billed, from order lines).

inventory_account_balance.description: Net balance of every account carrying an inventory role in use.

pending_outbox.description:
Posting requests still awaiting acknowledgement — a nonzero count
explains a gap that will close on its own.

stock_value.description: Total stock value across all levels (the engine's truth).

GrniRow.description: One open order line on the GRNI position.

qty.description: Received and not yet billed, order-line UoM.

unit_value.description: Effective PO price × exchange rate — base currency.

status.description: Always `healthy` when the host is serving.

IncomeStatement.description: The income statement over a period.

fractional.description: Whether quantities in this UoM may carry decimals (kg yes, unit no).

is_default.description: The warehouse documents prefill; at most one.

warehouse_type.description: standard|transit|scrap — only 'standard' is in use for now.

InvoiceHeader.description: A row of the invoice register.

description.description: Defaults from the order line's description or item name.

InvoiceStatus.description: Where a purchase invoice is in its lifecycle.

subtotal.description: Sum of line nets, invoice currency.

total.description: After header discounts and other charges, before tax.

ItemBody.description:
All writable item fields, shared by create and update (PUT semantics:
the body is the item's full intended state).

ItemType.description:
What kind of thing an item is, which decides whether the stock ledger
tracks it: stockable moves through the ledger, a consumable is expensed
on receipt, a service never has stock.

JournalEntryHeader.description: A row of the journal register (entry without its lines).

amount.description: Total debit (== total credit for a posted entry).

LedgerRowView.description: One stock ledger row with its document labels.

below_reorder.description: True when a reorder level is set and on_hand is at or below it.

reorder_level.description: Effective policy: the level row's override, else the item's.

changes.description: Only the fields that actually changed.

refresh_token.description:
Long-lived, single-use: exchange it at `POST /auth/token/refresh`
for a fresh pair.

tenant.description:
The tenant the session belongs to — send it as the tenant
header on subsequent requests. Null in single-tenant mode.

oneOf.description:
Password accepted; finish with `POST /auth/login/two-factor`
(send the tenant header there too).

The company mandates 2FA and this account has none yet: use the
token on the setup + confirm endpoints, then log in again.

The credentials matched accounts in more than one company: retry
with the tenant header set to the chosen one.

LogoUpload.description: Multipart body of the logo upload.

file.description:
The image file: png, jpg or webp, at most 1 MiB. SVG is refused —
it is a script container, and `/public` serves it same-origin.

MoveHeader.description: A row of the movement register (document without its lines).

batch_no.description:
The lot moved — required when the item tracks batches (receipts
and count-ups create the lot on first sight).

qty.description:
Always positive, in the item's stock UoM. For adjustments this is
the counted quantity (zero allowed).

serial_nos.description:
The serial units moved — required when the item tracks serials;
for adjustments, the units of the difference.

unit_cost.description:
Required per line when posting a receipt; optional cost override
for an adjustment counting up (required up from zero). Issues and
transfers must not send one.

MoveStatus.description: Where a movement document is in its lifecycle.

MoveType.description: The four kinds of stock movement document.

source.description:
Set when this document was generated by another (`module.doc:{id}`);
such movements reverse only through their source.

OrderHeader.description: A row of the order register.

effective_price.description: Unit price after the line discount — what receipts cost stock at.

OrderStatus.description: Where a purchase order is in its lifecycle.

subtotal.description: Sum of line nets, order currency.

total.description: After header discounts and other charges, before tax.

PeriodStatus.description:
A period's lifecycle: postings land only in `Open`; `Closed` finalises a
month (reversible); `Locked` is permanent.

debit.description: Non-negative; exactly one of debit/credit is set on a line.

last_price.description: Supplier currency; goods receipts maintain it.

currency.description: ISO 4217; purchase orders default to this.

on_hold.description: Blocks new purchase orders; in-flight documents finish.

Profile.description: The client-safe view of a user.

QueuedJobResponse.description: A background job was accepted onto a queue.

ReceiptHeader.description: A row of the receipt register.

batch_no.description:
The supplier's lot — required when the item tracks batches
(created on first sight, expiry from the item's shelf life).

serial_nos.description: The serial units received — required when the item tracks serials.

ReceiptStatus.description: Where a goods receipt is in its lifecycle.

move_id.description: The stock movement this receipt produced at post.

memo.description: What the money was for, e.g. "Office stationery".

payment_account_id.description: The asset account the money left (cash, bank, a petty-cash float).

reference.description: External document (a till slip, an invoice number).

tax_code_id.description: An input (purchase) tax code, when the spend carries recoverable tax.

currency.description:
The company's currency, a code from `GET /currencies`.
Ignored in single-tenant mode.

tenant_name.description:
Tenant (company) name: lowercase letters, digits, dashes.
Ignored in single-tenant mode.

ReorderOrderView.description: One draft order a run produced.

ReorderSkipView.description: One shortage a run could not order for, and why.

RequisitionHeader.description: A row of the requisition register.

RequisitionStatus.description: Where a purchase requisition is in its lifecycle.

order_id.description: The draft purchase order convert produced.

retention_days.description: Days to keep audit rows; null reverts to the system default.
The tenant's override, when one is set.

ReturnHeader.description: A row of the returns register.

batch_no.description: Required when the item tracks batches (the lot going back).

serial_nos.description: Required when the item tracks serials (the exact units going back).

ReturnStatus.description: Where a purchase return is in its lifecycle.

move_id.description: The outbound stock movement this return produced at post.

entry_date.description:
The reversal's date; defaults to today. Never before the original
entry's date, and its period must be open.
The reversal's date; defaults to today, never before the original.

RfqHeader.description: A row of the RFQ register.

RfqStatus.description: Where an RFQ is in its lifecycle.

order_id.description: The draft purchase order the award produced.

permissions.description: Explicit grants; static roles implicitly hold every permission.

SerialStatus.description: Where a serialized unit is in its life.

denied.description: Permissions denied even when a role grants them.

granted.description: Permissions granted beyond what the user's roles give.

StatementLine.description:
One account's contribution to a financial statement, at its natural-side
balance.

StatementSection.description: A titled group of statement lines with its subtotal (e.g. "Assets").

StatusResponse.description: Generic acknowledgement for operations without a richer result.

balance.description: Posted invoice totals in the supplier currency.

base_balance.description: The same, converted at each invoice's exchange rate.

SupplierScorecardRow.description:
One supplier's performance grades. Percentages and averages are
`None` when the window holds no data to compute them from.

avg_bill_days.description: Average days from order date to invoice date.

avg_lead_days.description: Average days from order date to goods receipt.

billed_value_base.description: Posted invoice line value at each invoice's rate — base currency.

invoices.description: Purchase invoices posted in the window.

on_time_pct.description:
Receipt lines arriving on or before their expected date, as a
percentage of lines that had one.

orders.description: Orders placed (submitted or beyond) in the window.

price_range_pct.description:
Average per-item spread of PO prices across receipts — how much
this supplier's pricing drifts order to order.

price_variance_pct.description:
Value-weighted percentage the supplier billed above (+) or below
(−) the PO price.

receipts.description: Goods receipts posted in the window.

received_value_base.description: Received quantity × PO price × rate — base currency.

rejection_pct.description: Rejected quantity over delivered quantity (accepted + rejected).

TaxDirection.description: Which side of the ledger a tax code lands on.

TenantChoice.description: One of the companies a set of credentials belongs to.

code.description: Authenticator code or a recovery code.

TwoFactorSetup.description:
Returned once from two-factor setup; the secret and URL feed the
authenticator app, the recovery codes are shown a single time.

address.description: Postal/street address (may be multi-line); blank clears it.

default_currency.description: A code from `GET /currencies`; null clears the default.

account_id.description: Present to change the linked account (may be null to clear it).

effective.description: Fully resolved: roles unioned, overrides applied, deny wins.

ValuationSummary.description: Stock value across warehouses — what the GL books as inventory.

items.description: Distinct items with stock on hand.
